item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Tower attempts to flee the test chamber but is restrained by the cabling attaching it to the monitor, keyboard and mouse."

Well, that's your +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oinkers has fan art!

  • The prolific SunnyClockwork did this piece as part of her Chinese Xodiac series. Awesome as always, Sunny.
  • Smileknife made this on their Tumblr. It's so cute!

item.title: Re: Why are the names not redacted?
item.content:encoded:
Redaction of any information, whether it's name, places, effects, anomalies, anything, is up to the Author. There is no site standard for redaction outside of don't redact info in the containment procedures. I can't remember there ever being a push to not have names present in articles except for maybe way back in 2012 or 2013, when the site had a hate for anything with a name because of author avatars.

From my own opinion, I really dislike when names are blackboxed as they really break the flow of reading for me, and look ugly on the page.

item.title: Why are the names not redacted?
item.content:encoded: I keep seeing this, am I out of the loop or has there been a format change of not redacting the names of individuals? Considering it's a test log by, what I assume is, the foundation they should be redacted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sweet and interesting and I love the non-binary character. I also am a fan of computing-related SCPs. Excellent work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that's something I chewed over, but ultimately, I wanted this article to be absolutely as concise as I could make it. I think the line about the pen handler covers it, and maybe gives an indication of the kind of place Area-12 is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's higher_being.oinc, actually. I'd prefer to leave the skip to speak for itself on this one, but I'll say that .oink is a hidden folder that contains uneditable files that aren't human-readable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the Mythbusters did prove that driving while sleep deprived is worse than driving while drunk, sooo…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I swear to God I was 100% sober for the entire process of writing it. Sleep deprived and stressed out of my brain writing an report worth 30% of a class, but fully sober.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: sends you to the astral plane, duh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy shit man, this stuff you can only come up with when you're high. Hell, after reading this I feel high. A pig that is able to fuse itself to a computer and send you to the astral plane? Whoa dude.

+1000000000000000000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what did .oink do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unique and interesting, a little computer-piglet. Concise as well, with all paragraphs adding something to the article.
I would like to see a bit more regarding containment policy other than just "it's kept with other animals"(then again, what would I know), but otherwise, really nice article.

+1 from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No probs. Congrats on the +100!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Z. Sorry for not attributing in the first place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually have a fairly significant personal thing against blackbars and redactions. I feel like sharing specific dates, names and locations can add a lot to an article in terms of character and flavour, and black boxing for the sake of black boxing takes away from that hugely.

I'll take a look at how the fusion dance is described though, that could probably have been cleared up.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Woooooooooo!
Hooray for good sweet teacup piglet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an incredibly minor gripe and has little to no bearing on the article itself but naming names in the discover of the SCP feels off, somehow

I know blanking out info is a really convenient trope in these things but in this case it feels off.

It could also maybe be clearer that the lil piglet becomes part of the computer tower? It comes across as a bit of a surprise that you calm the tower down and the SCP returns to existence, apparently?

This is still a solid SCP, I just want this one thing to make it more consistent with the overall feel of it being a regimented database of anomalous… nouns, I guess. It's definitely a good thing that my only complaints are with the semantics of execution; the core of the SCP, which is what really matters, is totally there and works.

item.title: Adorable
item.content:encoded: The title says it all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brendan doesn't have a doctorate, so the honorary doesn't apply. They bring a BSc in Zoology and a lot of enthusiasm to the table instead.

I think Oinkers' acquisition is laid out pretty clearly? I didn't feel the need to, like, make out a death report for Renard or describe what went down in the police station. I don't see how I could add to it in any way that would add meaning or interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noting that the image is from here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Arapawa_Pigglet.jpg

And is licensed under: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/deed.en

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't Parks be referred to as Dr. Instead of Mx?

Also, shouldn't there be a bit more history on the piglet and its acquisition?

Anyway, this was real cute. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops. Forgot to take those out >.<

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only gripe is that DARE doesn't need the periods in it. It's not an initialization for anything, but some versions of it have the name rendered in Kanji with the all-caps English name being a "translation". Other than that, it's still pretty solid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You have enough articles to make an author page!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2779 is a teacup piglet

Cute, I love piglets!

SCP-2779 is capable of interfacing with and becoming devices running x64 Windows Vista Home Basic and Home Premium.

ಠ_ಠ

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OINK, motherfucker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gender-neutral derivative of Mr/Mrs. Most commonly seen in the professional realm in the UK.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As cute as it is weird. I appreciate how short but effective the description is.

"Mx.": typo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the intent, yes. Brendan is non-binary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
IDK if this is what the author intended, but Mx. is also used as a gender neutral alternative to Ms. or Mr.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mx_(title)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm totally on board with gender neutral prefixes, but how should I be pronouncing Mx. in my head? Right now I'm going with "Mix Parks" which sounds fine to me, but could also be incorrect ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

Anyway, +1 for adorable computer piglet, but man do I love that readme! The idea of being able to think your way around the astral plane is nothing new, but the fact that in order to do so the kid has to think of his movement in terms of WASDRF is intriguing. In fact, all of this magic technology is somewhat reminiscent of Maxwellist/WAN stuff. Any connection?

And no-romance policy?! You've succeeded in throwing me for a loop in a single postscript.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Transhumanism of a sort, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is adorable.

One possible typo - "Mx. Parks wraps SCP-2779 its blanket and carries it out of the testing chamber" - should this be "Mx. Parks wraps SCP-2779 in its blanket"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mx. Parks requests the lights in the chamber be dimmed, then withdraws a blanket from their backpack and wraps it around the struggling computer tower. Mx. Parks withdraws an mp3 player from within their blouse pocket and begins to play calming music. Computer tower begins to calm down and accept apple slices from Mx. Parks. After approximately five minutes, computer tower beings to glow and resolve back into a non-anomalous computer tower and SCP-2779. Mx. Parks wraps SCP-2779 its blanket and carries it out of the testing chamber.

… D'aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaw!
+1 for Cute Computer-Pig, and another +1 for the implications of, if I'm reading this right, Transhumanism. Very nice and creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff Notice: This user has been permanently banned for site vandalism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: shouldn't the item number also have SCP- in it or is that just your style?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the fuck. What the fuck.

+1 (and +99 more if I could). this is the good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. I wanted the Foundation to sound a little out of touch with "pop group", but I definitely wouldn't want them to be inaccurate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Little quibble I want to address. The band is just called Gorillaz. There's no definitive article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something short and sweet.

Thanks to AbsentmindedNihilist, anqxr and Lex1nat0r for their help.

EDIT: Also, LilyFlower is cute and deserves praise. Go read Bumblebee Philharmonic Orchestra, and then comment specifically on how cute she is, with no reference to the skip whatsoever. Thanks.
